moduleReviews.content: Calculus is a nightmare if you skip classes! The professor REALLY deducts 5 points if you are late. Buying a secondhand textbook with previous notes highlighted saved my life.
Pay attention to the proof formats. If you skip steps in the exam, you get zero points even if the final answer is right. Highly recommend grabbing the study guide here.

This is a crucial core course that you'll use in almost all future classes! Prof. Ma Chuanxiang is super gentle and patient when answering questions. BUT WARNING: DO NOT whisper in class! Her voice is naturally soft, so if there's background noise, she has to raise her voice and will get REALLY angry. Homework is easy—just bundle it all and submit it to the study commissary near the end of the term. She grades incredibly fast; results are usually out the exact next day!And the assessment strycture is 40%for homework and attendence,60%for the final exam

The professor is super enthusiastic, outgoing, and loves chatting with students. Equipment tip: You MUST use DOSBox, so a Windows laptop is highly recommended! Mac users will face a ton of installation issues. This is a mandatory subject for grad school entrance exams, though our class doesn't go quite that deep. Theory class covers the architecture, while lab sessions in the computer room teach Assembly language. Assembly is hard to grasp—prepare to practice a LOT in your free time!

The assessment is 100% based on a group electronic report submitted on Moodle. Warning: the groups are completely random, so you might be teamed up with total strangers. Teamwork is crucial because your final individual grade heavily depends on the peer review percentage. Make sure you actually contribute to the main report!

You'll learn how to draw ERDs and UCDs, plus some solid SQL skills. The foreign lecturer's English accent is a bit hard to catch at first, but he explains concepts through stories, which makes it really fun. Huge shoutout to the Chinese tutor—super patient! We discussed a database problem late into the night and they still replied thoroughly.

I believe everyone will love this course! We use 'Processing' to draw and create fun mini-games. It really lets your imagination run wild. Every class has a lab task: theory first, then straight to the computer room to practice coding. Tip: The teacher speaks quite softly, so definitely grab a seat in the front row!

Assessment is 30% online midterm and 70% final mini-game project. BUT WARNING: You absolutely MUST submit a development log alongside your final game. If you forget the dev log, you will get a straight 0! Don't let your hard work go to waste.

modules.name: Advanced Mathematics
requirements: The course is challenging with a strict focus on understanding proofs and calculus concepts, making it tough if you fall behind.
Grading is strict, with point deductions for lateness and zero credit for skipped steps in exams, emphasizing precision and attendance.
Buy a secondhand textbook with highlighted notes and use the study guide to navigate the rigorous proof formats and avoid exam pitfalls.

modules.name: Data Structures
requirements: Data Structures (SE201) is a moderately difficult course requiring a solid foundation in programming and algorithms as prerequisites.
Attendance is mandatory with strict policies, and absences may affect grades unless excused in advance.
Exams include a midterm and a final, both typically consisting of multiple-choice, short-answer, and coding problems.
